lessons.title: HTTP, JSON, and the Spring MVC Request Lifecycle
topics: The request lifecycle: from `DispatcherServlet` through handler mapping to the controller
How HTTP bodies and JSON bind to method parameters and DTOs
Where untrusted input enters — the attack surface of a Spring endpoint
objects: Object: Lesson: HTTP, JSON, and the Spring MVC Request Lifecycle
Assessment: Quiz: HTTP, JSON, and the Spring MVC Request Lifecycle

lessons.title: Controllers, Request Mapping, and Input Validation
topics: `@RestController`, request mapping, and DTO-based request binding
Bean Validation (`@Valid`, constraint annotations) to enforce shape and bounds
Rejecting malformed or hostile requests before they reach business logic
objects: Object: Lesson: Controllers, Request Mapping, and Input Validation
Assessment: Quiz: Controllers, Request Mapping, and Input Validation

lessons.title: Secure REST Endpoint Design — Headers, CSRF, and CORS
topics: Security response headers and why they matter for an API
CSRF protection: when it applies to REST endpoints and how Spring enforces it
Configuring CORS as a least-privilege allow-list, not a wildcard
objects: Object: Lesson: Secure REST Endpoint Design — Headers, CSRF, and CORS
Assessment: Quiz: Secure REST Endpoint Design — Headers, CSRF, and CORS

lessons.title: Spring Security Essentials — Authentication and Authorization
topics: The Spring Security filter chain and where it sits in the request lifecycle
Authentication primitives: establishing who is calling
Authorization primitives: protecting endpoints by role and least privilege
objects: Object: Lesson: Spring Security Essentials — Authentication and Authorization
Assessment: Quiz: Spring Security Essentials — Authentication and Authorization

lessons.title: Secure Error Handling — No Stack-Trace Leakage
topics: How default error responses leak stack traces, types, and system internals
Centralized exception handling (`@ControllerAdvice` / `@ExceptionHandler`) for safe responses
Returning useful, non-revealing error payloads and correct status codes
objects: Object: Lesson: Secure Error Handling — No Stack-Trace Leakage
Assessment: Quiz: Secure Error Handling — No Stack-Trace Leakage

lessons.title: Secure-Endpoint Capstone Lab
topics: Build a REST endpoint that validates input, authenticates and authorizes callers, sets correct headers/CSRF/CORS, and handles errors safely
Audit the endpoint against the course's secure-coding controls
Demonstrate the endpoint resists a scripted set of attacks (bad input, unauthorized access, error-leak probes)
